rows.match_reasons: ["This project is an excellent fit for the student's strong programming skills (Python, R, Java) and experience in AI/ML (AI Engineering Intern, AI Teaching Bot). The student could contribute to developing and applying machine learning models for materials discovery, leveraging their data analysis and programming expertise."]
rows.project_name: Machine Learning for Materials Discovery and Design
rows.research_direction: Computational Materials Science, Machine Learning in Chemical Engineering
rows.description: Research in the de Pablo group focuses on using computational methods, including machine learning and molecular simulations, to understand and design new materials with desired properties. Projects often involve developing algorithms to predict material behavior or optimize synthesis pathways.
rows.requirements: Strong programming skills (Python preferred), interest in materials science and machine learning. Experience with data analysis libraries (Pandas, Matplotlib) is a plus.

rows.match_reasons: ["The student's background in AI (internship, project) and programming skills (Python, R, Java) are highly relevant for applying machine learning to chemical process optimization. Their experience with data analysis tools would be valuable for handling and interpreting process data."]
rows.research_direction: Applied Mathematics, Machine Learning for Complex Systems, Process Optimization
rows.description: Professor Kevrekidis's research involves using data-driven and machine learning techniques to understand, model, and optimize complex dynamical systems, including those found in chemical engineering processes. Projects could involve developing AI models for process control or parameter estimation.
rows.requirements: Proficiency in Python or MATLAB, strong mathematical background, interest in dynamic systems and machine learning.

rows.match_reasons: ["This project aligns well with the student's AI/ML experience and programming skills. They could contribute to developing predictive models for catalytic performance or exploring high-throughput data analysis from material characterization, leveraging their Python and data visualization expertise."]
rows.project_name: AI/ML for Understanding and Designing Catalytic Materials
rows.description: The Murray group synthesizes and characterizes colloidal nanocrystals and quantum dots for applications in catalysis, energy, and electronics. Projects could involve using machine learning to predict catalytic activity based on material properties or optimize synthesis conditions.
rows.requirements: Interest in materials science and catalysis, strong data analysis skills. Experience with Python and machine learning frameworks is highly desirable.
